lede: A look at March 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kyle Hamilton and Sharon Cherop, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Rachel Corrie
events.desc: American activist Rachel Corrie is killed in Rafah by being run over by an Israel Defense Forces bulldozer while trying to obstruct the demolition of a home.

events.title: Sofia Gubaidulina
events.desc: Sofia Gubaidulina's Johannes-Ostern is premiered, together with her earlier Johannes-Passion, at Hamburg's Michaeliskirche, performed by soloists, choir and orchestra from the Mariinsky Theatre combined with NDR choir and orchestra, conducted by Valery Gergiev.

events.title: 2001 Shijiazhuang bombings
events.desc: A series of bomb blasts in the city of Shijiazhuang, China kill 108 people and injure 38 others, the biggest mass murder in China in decades.

events.title: William Francis Buckley
events.desc: William Buckley, the CIA station chief in Lebanon, is kidnapped by Hezbollah; he later dies in captivity.

events.title: Aldo Moro
events.desc: Former Italian Prime Minister Aldo Moro is kidnapped; he is later murdered by his captors.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: My Lai massacre occurs; between 347 and 500 Vietnamese villagers are killed by American troops.

events.title: Flying Tiger Line Flight 739
events.desc: Flying Tiger Line Flight 739 disappears in the western Pacific Ocean with all 107 aboard missing and presumed dead.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Iwo Jima ends, but small pockets of Japanese resistance persist.

events.title: Long Parliament
events.desc: The Long Parliament of England is dissolved so as to prepare for the new Convention Parliament.

events.title: Samoset
events.desc: Samoset, an Abenaki, visits the settlers of Plymouth Colony and greets them, "Welcome, Englishmen! My name is Samoset."

births.name: Harry van Hoof
births.line: Dutch conductor, composer, and music arranger (died 2024)

deaths.text: Ksenija Pajčin, Serbian singer, dancer and model
Selma Lagerlöf, Swedish author and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Christian Augustus, Prince of Anhalt-Zerbst, Prince of Anhalt-Zerbst

observances.text: Christian feast day: Finian Lobhar (Finian the Leper)
Christian feast day: Blessed John Amias and Robert Dalby
